briefdescription.para: Creates an uninitialized Trait handle.
Enable touch-based long press detection.

detaileddescription.para: By default, the long press gesture detector is not created. Call this method to enable touch-based long press detection on demand. This is also called automatically when LongPressedSignal() is accessed.

briefdescription.para: Emitted when the pressed state changes.

detaileddescription.para: This signal is emitted when the view's pressed state changes due to touch or keyboard input. The pressed state is true when the user is interacting with the view (e.g., pressing down), and false when the interaction ends (e.g., releasing the touch or key).

briefdescription.para: Emitted when the pseudo disabled state changes.

detaileddescription.para: This signal is emitted when the pseudo disabled state is changed via SetPseudoDisabled(). The view is visually presented as disabled when in pseudo disabled state, but it still accepts user interaction to provide guidance or an alternative flow.

briefdescription.para: Emitted when the view is clicked.

detaileddescription.para: This signal is emitted when the view receives a tap gesture, indicating a click action. The click action is triggered when the user taps on the view.

simplesect.para: This signal may not be emitted if the one of LongPressed signal handler consumes the event (returns true).

briefdescription.para: Emitted when the view receives a long press gesture.

detaileddescription.para: This signal is emitted when the user performs a long press on the view. A long press is detected when the user presses and holds on the view for a specified duration.

simplesect.para: If the handler returns true to consume the event, the Clicked signal will not be emitted.
This signal is not emitted when ClickedKeyType is set to PRESS.

briefdescription.para: The boolean flag for pressed state.

simplesect.para: True if the object is pressed

briefdescription.para: Checks if the view is pseudo disabled state. The view is visually presented as disabled, but it still accepts user interaction in order to provide guidance or an alternative flow.

simplesect.para: True if the object is pseudo disabled

briefdescription.para: Sets the pseudo disabled state to the view. The view is visually presented as disabled, but it still accepts user interaction in order to provide guidance or an alternative flow.

parameterdescription.para: True if the object is pseudo disabled

briefdescription.para: Checks if clicking is currently allowed (not blocked).

simplesect.para: True if clicking is allowed, false if clicking is blocked

briefdescription.para: Sets whether clicking is allowed.

parameterdescription.para: True to allow clicking, false to block clicking

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the current key click policy.

simplesect.para: The active KeyClickPolicy for this button.

briefdescription.para: Configures the trigger timing for key-driven click events.

parameterdescription.para: The KeyClickPolicy to be applied (OnRelease, OnPress, or Disabled).

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

briefdescription.para: Gets the owner view.
This is called when touch input is received.
This is called when tap gesture is received.
Pressed changed event. override this method to handle pressed event.
Clicked event. override this method to handle clicked event.
LongPressed event. override this method to handle long pressed event.
Check if the key is for execution.
Internal implementation of Interaction trait.
